slots.rod: RIVERTEX Fenix 11'5" (Match Rod, 6.5-17.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium protects against Chain Pickerel if one takes the bait. Bloodworms cover 6 species including Warmouth ($170/kg!) — the highest $/kg fish at Quanchkin.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-7 ft in shallow swamp areas near cypress trees. Warmouth hold near thick vegetation and structure. At Cypress Dreams, try 3-4 ft near cypress knees.

issues: Minor: Titanium .004" (7 Lb) is light — upgrade to .005" (11 Lb) for better pickerel protection
improvements: CRITICAL FIX: Bloodworms replaces Flies — now covers Warmouth ($170/kg!), Bluegill, Black Crappie, White Crappie, Redear Sunfish, AND Smallmouth Buffalo (6 species vs 1!)
Hook downsized from #1/0 to #4 — properly sized for panfish mouths
WARMOUTH is your missing expedition fish — this bait change should catch it immediately
Warmouth at $170/kg is tied with Spotted Bass for highest $/kg at Quanchkin — passive gold mine
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Cypress Dreams or Pelican Hut for passive panfish/Warmouth income
Bloodworms also attracts Smallmouth Buffalo ($50/kg, up to 24.3 lb) — surprise bonus catches

slots.rod: SpookySpin 8'6" (Spinning Rod, Heavy, 5/7-1 3/4 Oz, 6.6-19.8 Lb, Mod Fast)
issues: Heavy rod is well-matched for Quanchkin's mid-size predators
Bullet Spinner attracts aggressive predators — flash and vibration in swamp water
19.8 Lb max line is borderline if Bowfin (19.8 lb) or Alligator Gar hits
improvements: Bullet Spinner is excellent for Spotted Bass ($170/kg, max 9.9 lb) — top income target for lure fishing
Also catches Largemouth Bass ($152/kg) and Chain Pickerel ($150/kg) — great income rod
Titanium leader protects against Chain Pickerel and Bowfin teeth
Cast near fallen trees, lily pads, and cypress knees — bass ambush from these structures
Steady retrieve with occasional rod twitches — triggers reactive strikes in murky swamp water

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast) — from home inventory
slots.terminal_tackle: JigHead 9g (1/3 Oz) #2/0 with Worm 3" or Shad 3"
issues: Light power rod (3-13 Lb) means big surprise hookups (Bowfin 19.8 lb, catfish) will test the setup
Titanium leader .005" (11 Lb) provides adequate pike/pickerel protection
improvements: MAJOR UPGRADE — downsized from Loki Heavy (5/7 Oz min) to JigWinner Light (1/6 Oz min)
9g (1/3 Oz) jighead with #2/0 hook stays in the strike zone at 2-4 ft instead of dragging bottom
Fast action gives excellent jig sensitivity — feel every tap from Spotted Bass ($170/kg) in shallow swamp
Smaller Worm 3" or Shad 3" matches the forage size in shallow cypress swamp water
JigWinner's 1/6-5/8 Oz range accepts jigs from ultralight to medium — versatile for all depths at Quanchkin
Hop the jig gently near cypress knees and lily pads — let it sink slowly on slack line, bass strike on the fall
This rod targets Spotted Bass ($170/kg) more effectively than the heavy Loki — finesse over power in shallow water
Titanium .005" upgraded from .004" for better Chain Pickerel bite protection

slots.rod: Heavy Chaser 9'10" SE (Bottom Rod, X-Heavy, Casting 6-8.5 Oz, up to 81 Lb) — from home inventory
slots.terminal_tackle: Sinker 7-8 Oz (from inventory), Catfish Hook #6/0, Simple Bell
issues: Sinker must be 6-8.5 Oz to match rod's casting weight — use your 7 Oz or 8 Oz sinkers from inventory
79 Lb line + 79.4 Lb drag is perfectly balanced — well-matched system
improvements: MASSIVE UPGRADE — from Big Ali feeder (16 Lb line) to Heavy Chaser bottom rod (79 Lb line)
79 Lb fluoro + 79.4 Lb drag means Flathead Catfish (77.2 lb) is at comfortable tension
Alligator Gar (109.1 lb) at ~75% tension — a tough fight but survivable
Purpose-built bottom rod designed for monster catfish and sturgeon — exactly what Quanchkin needs
Bait sits directly on the bottom where catfish and gar feed — the most natural presentation
Titanium .012" leader handles gar teeth and catfish whisker abrasion
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Down South or Swampy Open Space — cast to deepest channel (10-15+ ft)
PAIR WITH SLOT 7: Two monster-class passive rods — slot 4 on bottom, slot 7 at mid-depth float. Covers the entire water column for gar and catfish.
Night sessions: Both slots 4 and 7 soaking simultaneously = maximum monster fish exposure

slots.rod: Rivertex Zeus 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz, 18-46 Lb, Mod Fast)
issues: 41.2 Lb braid will be SEVERELY tested by Alligator Gar (109.1 lb) — expect very long fights at high tension
This is still your best shot at landing the monsters — no other rod comes close
Jig with Bony Shad is effective for all predators here
improvements: MONSTER FISH SETUP — the only rod capable of fighting Alligator Gar (109.1 lb!) and Flathead Catfish (77.2 lb)
81.5 Lb drag gives headroom — set to 25-30 Lb and let big fish run
Bony Shad 16cm on heavy jig targets all predators — bass, catfish, pickerel, gar, bowfin
Thick titanium .012" leader handles gar teeth — essential for Alligator Gar's saw-like mouth
Work the jig along deep channels and near submerged timber — Alligator Gar lurk near structure
If an Alligator Gar takes the jig, it will make long powerful runs — keep rod tip up, let drag work
Also use for Blue Catfish ($70/kg, 58.4 lb) — better line capacity than the feeder rod

leader_recommendation.why: Fluoro leader for panfish stealth. However, Flies only targets Bluegill at Quanchkin — very narrow coverage. This slot is underperforming at a 17-species location.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-5 ft near cypress knees and vegetation.

issues: Flies ONLY covers Bluegill ($70/kg) at Quanchkin Lake — wastes a slot at a 17-species location
Hook #1/0 is way too large for Bluegill (max 3.5 lb) and panfish
No titanium leader — if a Chain Pickerel takes this, fluoro .008" will snap
X-Series Slim Float is elegant but this slot needs a complete bait overhaul
improvements: CRITICAL: Switch bait to Bloodworms — covers Bluegill, Black Crappie, White Crappie, Redear Sunfish, Warmouth, AND Smallmouth Buffalo
Downsize hook to #4 or #6 — panfish at Quanchkin have small mouths
Warmouth ($170/kg) and Spotted Bass ($170/kg) are the top earners — don't miss Warmouth with wrong bait
Alternatively use Red Worms — covers Bluegill and Warmouth
Deploy on RodPod Trio for passive panfish income while actively jig fishing with slots 3 and 5

slots.rod: EquatorMatch 8'6" SE (Match Rod, 28.6-83.8 Lb, Moderate) — from home inventory
slots.leader: Fluorocarbon Leader .034" (83.8 Lb) — swap to Titanium .012" for gar teeth

leader_recommendation.material: Fluorocarbon .034" (83.8 Lb) for general use, swap to Titanium .012" for Alligator Gar
leader_recommendation.why: The EquatorMatch SE is a saltwater-class match rod that accepts line up to 83.8 Lb — nearly triple the MatchMonster's 30 Lb limit. Paired with the L'Impulsion (81.5 Lb drag) and 83.8 Lb fluoro, a 109 lb Alligator Gar drops from 100%+ tension to ~75% — a survivable fight. The Fluoro .034" leader provides massive abrasion resistance; swap to titanium when specifically targeting gar since their armored mouths can abrade even heavy fluoro.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 8-15 ft. Alligator Gar cruise at mid-depth near structure in deep swamp channels. At Down South or Swampy Open Space, try 10-12 ft near submerged timber. Gar often surface to gulp air — watch for rolling fish and cast near them.

issues: 83.8 Lb line on 81.5 Lb drag means the drag is now the bottleneck, not the line — much better balance
A 109 lb gar at ~75% tension is a long fight but landable with patience
The EquatorMatch's Moderate action absorbs head-shakes better than Fast action rods
improvements: DEDICATED ALLIGATOR GAR SETUP — 83.8 Lb line + 81.5 Lb drag is the strongest float rig possible from your inventory
EquatorMatch SE (28.6-83.8 Lb line weight) is the ONLY rod in your inventory that accepts line heavy enough for 109 lb fish on a float rig
Fluoro .034" (83.8 Lb) is nearly triple the old MatchMonster's 30 Lb mono — massive upgrade
L'Impulsion 12000 SE provides 81.5 Lb drag — set to 50-60 Lb and let gar run when they need to
Shiners on Catfish #6/0 is wiki-recommended for Alligator Gar — keep this bait/hook combo
Also handles Flathead Catfish (77.2 lb) and Blue Catfish (58.4 lb) with ease on this line
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Down South or Swampy Open Space — cast to deep channel (10-12 ft) and wait

GAR FIGHTING TIPS: When a gar takes the float under, count to 3 before setting hook — they hold prey sideways first then turn to swallow. Set drag to 50-60 Lb. Let them run on the initial surge. Keep rod tip high. Expect 10-20 minute fights on trophy fish.
Note: L'Impulsion is shared with slot 5 (Zeus) — equip on whichever slot you're actively targeting. Use Zeus for active jigging, EquatorMatch for passive float soaking.

gear.boat: Garry Scott Draco Bass Boat (NOT USABLE — no boat fishing at Quanchkin Lake)
notes: Draco Bass Boat equipped but Quanchkin Lake does NOT allow boat fishing — shore only
Luminox Headlamp — CRITICAL for night sessions. Catfish, gar, and bowfin are most active after dark.
RodPod Trio — deploy with slots 1 (panfish), 4 (feeder/catfish), and 7 (heavy predator float) for triple passive income
FishStronghold M Plus — fill with Spotted Bass ($170/kg) and Largemouth Bass ($152/kg) before selling

groundbait.status: MyFeeder recipe — Catfish Destroyer + BigBeam Spicy + Oilcake-Pele
notes: Catfish Destroyer base is PERFECT for Quanchkin — targets bottom fish including catfish, gar, and drum
BigBeam Spicy aroma adds scent in murky swamp water where catfish hunt by smell
Deploy with Big Ali feeder (slot 4) in deep water for maximum catfish attraction

passive_fishing.best_stand_spot: Down South or Swampy Open Space
passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Deep swamp channels where Alligator Gar, catfish, and bass hold. The open areas allow good rod stand placement with clear sightlines to all 3 floats. Deep water access from shore is essential for monster fish targeting.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast spinning rods (slots 2, 3, 5) along cypress trees, fallen logs, and lily pad edges near your rod stand. Stay within 50 ft to react to float bites — Alligator Gar strikes are violent and need immediate response.
strategy: Deploy RodPod Trio with slots 1, 4, and 7 — panfish, feeder, and heavy predator float covering all sizes
Slot 4 (Big Ali feeder + Shiners + #6/0): Cast to deepest water — catfish and gar magnet with groundbait
Slot 7 (EquatorMatch SE + Shiners + #6/0 + 83.8 Lb fluoro): Cast to deep channel at 10-12 ft — DEDICATED ALLIGATOR GAR float rig
Slot 1 (Fenix + Flies): Near vegetation at 3-5 ft — SWITCH TO BLOODWORMS for broader panfish coverage
Actively fish slot 5 (Zeus + heavy jig) for Alligator Gar and Flathead Catfish near structure
Alternate with slot 2 (Bullet Spinner) for Spotted Bass ($170/kg) and Largemouth Bass ($152/kg) income
Slot 3 (Loki + jig) as backup active rod — different action than the Bullet Spinner
ALLIGATOR GAR ALERT: When slot 7's float goes under, count to 3 (gar hold prey sideways first), then SET HOOK HARD. Drag at 50-60 Lb. Let the gar run. Expect 10-20 min fights on trophy fish.

NIGHT STRATEGY: Focus ALL rods on big fish. Deploy slots 4 and 7 on stand — slot 7 (EquatorMatch 83.8 Lb) is your best chance at a trophy Alligator Gar after dark. Actively jig slot 5 (Zeus) in deep channels. Catfish and gar activity peaks 2x after dark.

NOTE: L'Impulsion 12000 SE reel is shared between slot 5 (Zeus jig) and slot 7 (EquatorMatch float). Equip on slot 7 for passive gar hunting, or slot 5 for active jigging. Swap as needed between sessions.
income_estimate: Spotted Bass ($170/kg, max 9.9 lb) — top $/kg predator. $762 per trophy. Active Bullet Spinner.
Warmouth ($170/kg, max 1.8 lb) — same $/kg, small fish. $139 per fish. Passive float with Bloodworms.
Largemouth Bass ($152/kg, max 12.1 lb) — up to $833 per trophy. Active jig/spinner.
Chain Pickerel ($150/kg, max 8.4 lb) — up to $571 per trophy. Active spinner/jig.
Alligator Gar ($40/kg but 109.1 lb max!) — up to $1,980 per trophy + MASSIVE XP! EquatorMatch float rig (slot 7) with 83.8 Lb line is your best shot.
Flathead Catfish ($40/kg, max 77.2 lb) — up to $1,400 per trophy. Feeder + Shiners at night.
Blue Catfish ($70/kg, max 58.4 lb) — up to $1,855 per trophy. Feeder + Shiners at night.
Passive income: $500-$1,500/hr from stand rods (panfish + catfish + gar hits)
Active income: $800-$2,000/hr from spinning bass and pickerel
Night catfish sessions: $1,000-$3,000/hr from big catfish and gar
Combined day: $1,300-$3,500/hr. Night: $2,000-$5,000/hr. Best income potential yet.

Fish all 6 spots for location discovery XP — Cypress Dreams, Down South, Good Ol' Tobacco, Pelican Hut, Swampy Open Space, Upside Down
Night fishing unlocks peak catfish and gar activity — huge XP potential from trophy catches
Switch one float rod to Bloodworms to quickly complete 5+ additional species for first-catch bonuses

Largemouth Bass at $152/kg (max 12.1 lb) — up to $833 per trophy
Chain Pickerel at $150/kg (max 8.4 lb) — strong bonus income from spinning
Blue Catfish at $70/kg (max 58.4 lb) — up to $1,855 per trophy! Best total $/fish for catfish
Fill keepnet with Spotted Bass + Largemouth Bass for highest $/kg average
Night sessions targeting Blue Catfish and Flathead Catfish are extremely profitable
Quanchkin costs $6,800 travel + $1,500/day extend — high-value species easily cover this
priority_changes: DONE: Slot 1 switched to Bloodworms + Hook #4 — unlocks Warmouth ($170/kg!), Redear Sunfish, Black/White Crappie, Smallmouth Buffalo (6 species!)
DONE: Slot 3 swapped to JigWinner 8'10" SE with 9g #2/0 jighead + Worm 3" — light jig stays in strike zone in shallow swamp
DONE: Slot 4 upgraded to Heavy Chaser 9'10" SE + Fileur 11000 SE (79 Lb line) — proper monster fish bottom rig
Bring Semolina Balls or Dough Balls for Golden Shiner species completion (swap onto slot 6 briefly)
